title: 10 best herbal tinctures for jaundice

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Milk thistle tinctures helps with jaundice because of its potent antioxidant properties, which help protect the liver from damage caused by this condition. Jaundice occurs when bile builds up in the blood due to liver dysfunction, causing yellowing of the skin and eyes. Milk thistle's silymarin extract has been shown to reduce liver inflammation and promote regeneration, allowing the organ to function more effectively and eliminate bilirubin, a key factor in jaundice. As a result, milk thistle tinctures may help alleviate symptoms of jaundice and support overall liver health.
remedy_properties: Silymarin: A flavonoid complex that helps protect liver cells from damage and promotes their regeneration, reducing inflammation and promoting healing in cases of jaundice.
Flavonoids: Which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, helping to reduce oxidative stress and alleviate symptoms such as itching and discomfort associated with jaundice.
Phenolic acids: That help to scavenge free radicals and reduce liver damage by chelating heavy metals and other toxic substances that may contribute to the development of jaundice.
remedy_constituents: Silymarin: Silymarin is a flavonoid complex composed of silibinin, silidianin, and silicristin, which helps with jaundice by protecting liver cells from damage, enhancing liver function, and promoting the excretion of bilirubin.

Silibinin: Silibinin is a major component of silymarin, which helps with jaundice by inhibiting the uptake of bilirubin by liver cells, reducing oxidative stress, and enhancing the activity of liver enzymes responsible for bilirubin metabolism.

Isosilicristin: Isosilicristin is another component of silymarin, which helps with jaundice by inhibiting the conjugation of bilirubin in the liver, promoting its excretion, and protecting against oxidative damage caused by excessive bilirubin levels.
remedy_parts: Seeds: The seeds are primarily used due to their high concentration of silymarin, a flavonoid complex that helps reduce liver inflammation and improve liver function.
Leaves: The leaves are used because they contain a similar concentration of silymarin as the seeds, making them a viable alternative for treating jaundice.

Flowers: The flowers are used for their medicinal properties, including their ability to reduce inflammation and improve liver function, although they may not contain as high a concentration of silymarin as the seeds and leaves.
remedy_recipe: Harvest silybum marianum flowers and seeds in full bloom and dry them thoroughly for 24 hours.
Combine 1 part dried silybum marianum with 2 parts 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place and shake it gently every day for 2 weeks.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into another clean glass jar and discard the solids.
Label and store the silybum marianum tincture in a cool dark place for up to 6 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion tinctures helps with jaundice because of its potent liver-supporting properties. The plant's active compounds, such as taraxasterol and inulin, have been shown to enhance bile production and flow, which can help alleviate the yellowing of skin and eyes characteristic of jaundice. Additionally, dandelion's antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects may also contribute to its therapeutic benefits by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the liver, promoting a healthier environment for digestion and metabolism.
remedy_properties: Choleretic and cholagogue actions: Dandelion tincture stimulates the liver to produce more bile, which helps to remove bilirubin from the bloodstream and reduce jaundice symptoms.

Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties: Dandelion's antioxidants help protect liver cells from damage caused by free radicals, while its anti-inflammatory compounds reduce inflammation that can contribute to jaundice.
Bile duct stimulant activity: Dandelion tincture stimulates the contraction of bile ducts, which helps to increase bilirubin excretion and alleviate symptoms such as yellowing of the skin and eyes.
remedy_constituents: Phenolic acids: They help with jaundice by supporting liver function, reducing oxidative stress, and promoting the excretion of bilirubin.
Flavonoids: They help with jaundice by improving liver enzyme activity, enhancing bile production, and reducing inflammation in the liver and gallbladder.
Polysaccharides: They help with jaundice by stimulating the immune system, reducing inflammation, and promoting the growth of beneficial gut bacteria.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Taraxacum officinale are used to make tinctures for jaundice due to their high content of saponins and flavonoids, which have hepatoprotective properties.
Roots: The roots of Taraxacum officinale are used to make tinctures for jaundice as they contain a high amount of inulin, a prebiotic that helps stimulate the liver and promote bile production.

Flowers: The flowers of Taraxacum officinale are used to make tinctures for jaundice because they are rich in flavonoids and carotenoids, which have antioxidant properties that help reduce liver inflammation and promote liver function.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh taraxacum officinale roots and leaves in the morning after the dew has evaporated and dry them using a food dehydrator.
Combine 100g of dried taraxacum officinale roots and leaves with 500ml of 95% ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-3 weeks with occasional shaking of the jar.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into a separate container discarding the solids and squeezing the cheesecloth to extract as much liquid as possible.
Filter the liquid through a coffee filter or a paper filter to remove any remaining particles and store the tincture in a dark glass bottle.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice tinctures helps with jaundice because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The flavonoids present in licorice root have been shown to reduce liver inflammation and improve bile flow, thereby helping to eliminate bilirubin from the blood. Additionally, licorice's glycrrhizin component has been found to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines, which can contribute to jaundice symptoms such as itching and fatigue. By addressing these underlying issues, herbal licorice tinctures may help alleviate the discomfort associated with jaundice.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: This compound has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help reduce liver inflammation and oxidative stress associated with jaundice.
Licoricidin: This bioactive compound has been found to exhibit immunomodulatory properties, which can help regulate the immune response and prevent further liver damage in cases of jaundice.

Liquiritigenin: This flavonoid has been shown to have antiviral and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help combat viral hepatitis infections that often lead to jaundice, as well as reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the liver.
remedy_constituents: Glycyrrhizic acid: This triterpenoid saponin helps with jaundice by inhibiting the conjugation of bilirubin, thereby reducing its levels in the blood and alleviating symptoms.

Flavonoids (specifically, quercetin and isorhapontigenin): These polyphenolic compounds possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce liver inflammation and oxidative stress, ultimately contributing to the treatment of jaundice.
Licoricidin: This flavanone glycoside has been shown to possess hepatoprotective properties, which help to protect the liver from damage, reduce bilirubin levels, and alleviate jaundice symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are the primary part of the plant used for medicinal purposes due to their high concentration of glycyrrhizin, an anti-inflammatory compound.
Leaves: Leaves of the plant contain glycosides, including glycyrrhizin, which aid in reducing liver inflammation and promoting bile production.
Barks: The bark of the plant contains glycosides and other compounds that help alleviate liver and digestive issues associated with jaundice.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh glycyrrhiza glabra roots in early summer when the plant is in full bloom.
Clean and chop the roots into small pieces using a sharp knife about 2 inches long.
Combine the chopped roots with 80% ethanol in a ratio of 1:5 by weight and steep for 2 weeks.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth to remove the solids and discard the solids.
Filter the liquid through a 0.2 micron filter to produce a clear tincture.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric tinctures helps with jaundice because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Curcumin, a key compound in turmeric, has been shown to reduce liver inflammation and improve bilirubin metabolism, which can help alleviate symptoms of jaundice such as yellowing of the skin and eyes. Additionally, turmeric's antioxidant activity helps protect the liver from damage caused by free radicals, promoting overall liver health and function. This natural approach can provide relief from jaundice symptoms and support the body's natural healing process.
remedy_properties: Curcuminoids: Turmeric tincture contains curcuminoids, which have potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects that help reduce liver damage and inflammation associated with jaundice.

Bioavailability-boosting Piperine: Turmeric tincture also contains piperine, a bioactive compound found in black pepper that enhances the absorption of curcuminoids by up to 2,000%, making it more effective in treating jaundice symptoms.

Hepatoprotective Lignans: Turmeric tincture's lignan content has been shown to protect and regenerate liver cells, helping to reduce bilirubin accumulation in the blood and alleviate symptoms of jaundice.
remedy_constituents: Curcumin: Curcumin, a polyphenolic compound, has potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate jaundice symptoms by reducing liver inflammation and improving liver function.

Demethoxycurcumin (DMC): DMC, another polyphenolic compound in Curcuma longa, has been shown to have hepatoprotective properties, which help in reducing liver damage and improving liver function in cases of jaundice.

Caryophyllene: Caryophyllene, a sesquiterpene, has been found to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help in reducing liver inflammation and improving liver function, thereby alleviating jaundice symptoms.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rich in bioactive compounds like curcumin, which has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help alleviate jaundice symptoms.
Roots: Contain volatile oils and other bioactive compounds that aid in the treatment of jaundice by reducing inflammation and promoting liver health.
Seeds: Are a rich source of curcumin and other beneficial compounds that help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation, which can contribute to jaundice.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 kilogram of fresh curcuma longa rhizomes in the early morning when the dew is still present.
Clean the rhizomes thoroughly with a soft brush to remove dirt and debris from the surface.
Chop the rhizomes into small pieces and macerate them in a 1:5 ratio with a neutral solvent like vodka for 14 days.
Strain the liquid mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean container and discard the solids.
Filter the tincture through a coffee filter and store it in a dark glass bottle in a cool place for up to 5 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow tinctures helps with jaundice because it coats and soothes the mucous membranes, reducing inflammation and irritation in the digestive tract. The herb's mucilaginous properties also help to bind to bilirubin, a yellow pigment produced during liver processing of old red blood cells, and facilitate its elimination from the body. This can aid in relieving symptoms such as jaundiced skin and eyes, abdominal discomfort, and pale stools characteristic of jaundice. By promoting healthy digestion and liver function, marshmallow tinctures may help alleviate jaundice symptoms and support overall liver health.
remedy_properties: Mucilages: Marshmallow tincture contains mucilages, which form a protective film on the gut lining, soothing and calming irritated tissues and helping to reduce inflammation and discomfort associated with jaundice.

Demulcent properties: The demulcent properties of marshmallow tincture help to coat and protect the digestive tract, reducing irritation and inflammation caused by bile salts and other toxins that can exacerbate jaundice symptoms.

Antioxidant activity: Marshmallow tincture has antioxidant properties that help to neutralize free radicals and reduce oxidative stress, which can contribute to liver damage and worsening of jaundice symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Gallic acid: Gallic acid helps with jaundice by its antioxidant properties, which may reduce oxidative stress and inflammation associated with liver damage and bile duct obstruction.
Flavonoids: Flavonoids, such as kaempferol and quercetin, help with jaundice by their hepatoprotective effects, which may protect liver cells from damage and improve liver function.

Mucilages (Polysaccharides): Mucilages in Althaea officinalis help with jaundice by providing a soothing effect on the gastrointestinal tract and promoting the elimination of bile and other toxins, which may help reduce jaundice symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are commonly used due to their high concentration of mucilages, which help soothe and protect the digestive system.
Stems: The stems are also utilized for their mucilaginous properties, which aid in reducing inflammation and promoting healing in the gastrointestinal tract.
Leaves: The leaves are used for their anti-inflammatory and soothing effects, which can help alleviate symptoms associated with jaundice.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30 grams of fresh roots of althaea officinalis and clean them thoroughly with water.
Chop the roots into small pieces and combine with 60% ethanol or glycerin in a 1:5 ratio.
Steep the mixture in a dark glass container for 2-3 weeks, shaking it daily.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth and discard the solids, retaining 250 milliliters of the tincture.
Transfer the tincture to a clean glass bottle and store it in a cool dark place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Stinging nettle tinctures helps with jaundice because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which aid in reducing liver damage and improving bile flow. The herbal extract contains compounds that have been shown to decrease bilirubin levels in the blood, a common symptom of jaundice. Additionally, nettle's natural choleretic effects help stimulate bile production and release, allowing for more effective removal of toxins from the body and promoting overall liver health and function.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Stinging nettle's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce liver inflammation and damage associated with jaundice, promoting a speedy recovery.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidant-rich constituents in stinging nettle tincture help neutralize free radicals and oxidative stress that can exacerbate liver dysfunction and bilirubin buildup in jaundiced individuals.

Hepatoprotective properties: Stinging nettle's compounds have been shown to protect the liver from damage by reducing bile duct obstruction, improving liver function, and promoting the excretion of bile pigments responsible for jaundice.
remedy_constituents: Isorhapontigenin: This flavonoid has been shown to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help protect the liver from damage and improve its function in cases of jaundice.
Ursolic acid: This triterpenoid has been demonstrated to have hepatoprotective effects, potentially reducing liver inflammation and improving bile flow, which can help alleviate symptoms of jaundice.

Quercetin: This flavonoid has been found to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help reduce liver damage and improve liver function, potentially alleviating jaundice symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They contain a high concentration of anthocyanins, which help to reduce liver inflammation and promote the removal of bilirubin from the blood.
Stems: They contain a compound called ursolic acid, which has been shown to improve liver function and reduce jaundice symptoms.

Roots: They have been traditionally used to stimulate liver function, reduce inflammation, and promote the elimination of toxins from the body, making them a valuable ingredient in jaundice tinctures.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh leaves of the herb in the early morning after the dew has evaporated and before midday sun exposure.
Dry the harvested leaves in a single layer on paper bags or wire racks for 7 to 10 days.
Combine 100 grams of dried leaves with 700 milliliters of 80 proof ethanol in a glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 to 3 weeks shaking the jar daily.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle discarding the solids and label the tincture.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree tinctures helps with jaundice because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The tannins present in the tincture have been traditionally used to reduce inflammation and congestion in the liver, which can alleviate symptoms of jaundice such as yellowing of the skin and eyes. Additionally, the Maidenhair tree's flavonoids help to protect the liver from damage caused by free radicals, promoting healthy liver function and supporting the body's natural detoxification processes. This holistic approach can effectively alleviate the discomfort associated with jaundice and promote overall well-being.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Ginkgo biloba's antioxidant properties help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the liver, which can contribute to jaundice symptoms.

Choleretic effects: The tincture's choleretic properties stimulate the release of bile from the liver, helping to improve digestion and reduce bilirubin levels in the blood, a common symptom of jaundice.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Ginkgo biloba's anti-inflammatory compounds may help reduce inflammation and damage to liver tissue caused by conditions like hepatitis or cirrhosis that can lead to jaundice.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These compounds, specifically bilobalide and ginkgolides, have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce liver inflammation and oxidative stress associated with jaundice.

Terpenoids: Terpenoids like bilobalide and ginkgolides also exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help protect liver cells from damage and promote liver regeneration in cases of jaundice.

Bilobalide: This specific terpenoid has been shown to have hepatoprotective effects by reducing liver damage and inflammation, and also by improving liver function and bile flow, which can help alleviate symptoms of jaundice.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in flavonoids and terpenoids, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help alleviate jaundice symptoms.
Seeds: They contain bilobalide and ginkgolides, which are thought to improve liver function and reduce jaundice severity.
Barks: They contain ginkgolides and bilobalide, which are believed to have hepatoprotective properties, helping to protect the liver and alleviate jaundice symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest ginkgo biloba leaves from mature trees in late summer or early fall for optimal potency and flavor.
Clean and dry the ginkgo biloba leaves to prevent contamination and spoilage.
Combine 250 grams of dried ginkgo biloba leaves with 750 milliliters of 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 4 to 6 weeks to extract the bioactive compounds.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or fine mesh into another clean glass container and discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sweet briar tinctures helps with jaundice because of its unique composition, which includes flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other bioactive compounds. These compounds have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to reduce bilirubin levels in the blood, thereby alleviating the symptoms of jaundice. Additionally, sweet briar tinctures have been traditionally used to stimulate digestion, improve liver function, and enhance bile flow, all of which can contribute to a faster recovery from jaundice. remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Sweet briar's antioxidants help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the liver, which can exacerbate jaundice symptoms like yellowing of the skin and eyes.

Choleretic properties: Sweet briar's choleretic compounds stimulate bile production and flow, which helps to remove bilirubin from the blood and relieve jaundice symptoms like dark urine and pale stools.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Sweet briar's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce liver inflammation and injury, which can contribute to jaundice in some cases, and also help alleviate associated symptoms like abdominal pain and discomfort.
remedy_constituents: Anthocyanins: These powerful antioxidants help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation, which can contribute to the development and severity of jaundice.
Flavonoids: These compounds have hepatoprotective properties, helping to protect and repair liver cells damaged by toxins or diseases that can cause jaundice.
Quercetin: This flavonoid has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help reduce liver inflammation and oxidative stress, alleviating jaundice symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used due to their hepatoprotective properties, which can help protect the liver and alleviate symptoms of jaundice.
Roots: Utilized for their galactagogues and antispasmodic properties, which can aid in milk production and provide relief from liver-related discomfort.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 pounds of rosa rubiginosa flowers during peak bloom for optimal potency.
Clean and dry the flowers thoroughly in a well-ventilated area for 2 hours.
Steep 1 part of the dried flowers in 2 parts of 80 proof vodka for 4 weeks.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth and discard the solids after 2 days.
Store the tincture in dark glass bottles at room temperature for 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile tinctures helps with jaundice because of its soothing and calming properties, which can ease digestive discomfort and reduce inflammation in the liver. The antioxidant-rich properties of chamomile also help to protect the liver from further damage and promote its natural function. Additionally, chamomile's gentle sedative effects can help to calm irritability and anxiety often associated with jaundice, allowing for a more peaceful recovery process. By promoting overall liver health and comfort, herbal chamomile tinctures can be a valuable natural remedy for managing jaundice symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Chamomile tincture's anti-inflammatory compound, apigenin, helps reduce inflammation in the liver and bile ducts, which can alleviate symptoms of jaundice such as yellowing of the skin and eyes.

Antioxidant property: Chamomile's antioxidant compounds, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, neutralize free radicals that can damage liver cells and exacerbate jaundice, promoting healthy liver function.

Sothothic property: Chamomile tincture's soothing and calming effect on the digestive system helps to reduce nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain associated with jaundice, promoting a sense of well-being and comfort.
remedy_constituents: Apigenin: This flavonoid helps with jaundice by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the liver, thereby promoting the elimination of bilirubin.
Apiole: This sesquiterpene lactone has anti-inflammatory and hepatoprotective properties, which help to soothe liver cells and reduce bilirubin levels.
Bisabolol: This sesquiterpene alcohol has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate liver damage and promote the removal of bilirubin from the body.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers are the most commonly used part for making tinctures to treat jaundice due to their high concentration of apigenin and other flavonoids with anti-inflammatory properties.
Leaves: The leaves of Matricaria chamomilla contain flavonoids and other compounds that help to reduce inflammation and promote liver function, making them a suitable choice for tinctures.
Roots: The roots of the plant contain sesquiterpenes and other compounds that exhibit hepatoprotective properties, helping to protect the liver and treat jaundice.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh matricaria chamomilla flowers in the morning when they are at their peak potency for 1-2 weeks.
Clean and dry the harvested flowers by spreading them out in a single layer in a warm dark room for 7-10 days.
Chop the dried flowers into small pieces using a sharp blade to increase their surface area for extraction.
Combine the chopped flowers with a neutral carrier oil such as grapeseed oil in a ratio of 1:3 flowers to oil by weight for 2-4 weeks.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter to separate the oil from the plant material and discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Blueberry tinctures helps with jaundice because of its unique properties that can help to break down and remove bilirubin, a yellow pigment responsible for the characteristic yellowing of skin and eyes. The antioxidants and anthocyanins present in blueberries work synergistically to increase bile production and flow, reducing bilirubin levels and alleviating symptoms. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory properties of blueberry tinctures may help soothe the liver and reduce inflammation, further contributing to its potential benefits in managing jaundice.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Blueberries' rich antioxidant content helps neutralize free radicals and reduce oxidative stress, which can contribute to liver damage and jaundice.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The flavonoids present in blueberry tincture have anti-inflammatory effects, which may help alleviate symptoms of jaundice such as itching, pain, and inflammation.

Liver-protective properties: Some studies suggest that blueberry extract may possess liver-protective activity by inhibiting the formation of liver damage-inducing compounds and promoting antioxidant defenses in the liver.
remedy_constituents: Anthocyanins: These powerful antioxidants help alleviate jaundice by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the liver, promoting its recovery and function.
Tannins: Tannins in bilberry tinctures can help bind to and remove bilirubin, a pigment responsible for jaundice, from the bloodstream, thereby reducing its levels and symptoms.

Vaccinium myrtillus phenolic acids (e.g., ferulic acid): These compounds have been shown to have hepatoprotective properties, helping to repair liver damage and improve liver function, which can contribute to alleviating jaundice.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are used due to their anthocyanin content, which has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that may help alleviate jaundice symptoms.
Fruits: The fruits, particularly the blueberries, are used for their rich antioxidant content, which may help reduce liver inflammation and promote overall liver health.
Roots: The roots are used due to their potential to stimulate liver function and promote the removal of bilirubin, a key component in jaundice diagnosis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of fresh vaccinium myrtillus leaves and berries by selecting ripe dark purple fruits.
Chop the harvested vaccinium myrtillus leaves and berries into small pieces using a sharp knife.
Combine the chopped vaccinium myrtillus leaves and berries with 500ml of 40% ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 4-6 weeks to allow infusion and extraction to occur.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean container and discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger tinctures helps with jaundice because it possesses anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that aid in reducing bilirubin levels in the blood. Bilirubin is a yellow pigment produced by the breakdown of old red blood cells, which can accumulate and cause jaundice. Ginger's bioactive compounds, such as gingerol and shogaol, have been shown to enhance liver function, promote bile flow, and reduce oxidative stress, thereby helping to alleviate symptoms of jaundice, including yellowing of the skin and eyes.
remedy_properties: Digestive Stimulant Property: Ginger tincture's ability to stimulate digestion can help alleviate symptoms of jaundice such as nausea and vomiting, which can exacerbate liver function impairment.
Anti-Inflammatory Property: Ginger's anti-inflammatory compounds, particularly gingerols and shogaols, may reduce inflammation in the liver and bile ducts, which is a common cause of jaundice.

Antioxidant Property: Ginger tincture's antioxidant activity can help neutralize free radicals that may contribute to oxidative stress and damage in the liver, thereby reducing the severity of jaundice symptoms.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rich in bioactive compounds, rhyzomes have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making them effective in reducing liver inflammation and improving bile flow for jaundice treatment.

Roots: As the primary storage organ of ginger, roots contain high levels of volatile oils that help stimulate digestion, relieve nausea, and reduce fever, all symptoms commonly associated with jaundice.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ginger roots of high quality in the morning for optimal potency.
Wash the harvested ginger roots thoroughly with clean water and pat dry immediately.
Chop the cleaned ginger roots into small pieces and combine them with 80% vodka or ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Allow the mixture to macerate for 14 days, shaking the jar daily for optimal extraction.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle, discarding the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marijuana tinctures helps with jaundice because they contain a unique combination of cannabinoids, flavonoids, and terpenes that work synergistically to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the liver. The anti-inflammatory properties of herbal marijuana tinctures can help to alleviate the discomfort and pain associated with jaundice, while also promoting the body's natural healing processes. Pain relief properties: CBD has been shown to have analgesic and anxiolytic effects, which could help manage symptoms such as nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain associated with jaundice.

Seeds: Marijuana seeds are rich in fatty acids, antioxidants, and other nutrients that can help support liver health and reduce the severity of jaundice symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry 28 grams of high-quality cannabis sativa flowers for optimal potency.
Crush dried flowers into fine powder using a mortar and pestle, yielding approximately 3 ounces.
Mix crushed flowers with 120 proof vodka in a dark glass jar at a ratio of 1:5 by weight.
Allow mixture to steep in a cool, dark place for 6 weeks, shaking the jar daily for 10 minutes each.
Strain and filter tincture through cheesecloth or coffee filters into a clean glass bottle for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Angelica tinctures helps with jaundice because it possesses potent anti-inflammatory properties that effectively reduce liver inflammation, a common underlying cause of this condition. The tincture's bitter compounds stimulate digestive processes, improving bile flow and excretion, which helps to eliminate bilirubin from the blood. Choleretic properties: Angelica's choleretic compounds stimulate bile production and flow, helping to improve liver function and remove excess bilirubin from the blood, which is often elevated during jaundice.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh angelica archangelica roots and leaves, washing them thoroughly to remove dirt and debris.
Chop the cleaned plant material into small pieces, then mix with 25% of its weight in vodka or high-proof grain alcohol for maceration.
Store the mixture in a dark glass jar at room temperature for 14 days to allow for maximum extraction.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding the solids and reserving the tincture.
Transfer the tincture to smaller dark glass bottles, storing them in the refrigerator to slow down oxidation and preserve potency.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dry larrea tridentata leaves and stems for maximum potency.
Chop the harvested material into small pieces to increase surface area exposure.
Combine one part chopped larrea tridentata with two parts vodka in a clean glass jar.
Allow the mixture to steep at room temperature for 6 weeks, shaking daily.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth and discard the solids, then store the tincture in a dark glass bottle.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Comfrey tinctures helps with jaundice because its active ingredients, such as allantoin and rosmarinic acid, have potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. These compounds help to reduce liver inflammation and promote the healing of damaged liver cells. Additionally, comfrey's saponins and flavonoids may also enhance liver function by improving bile flow and reducing bilirubin levels in the blood, thus alleviating jaundice symptoms such as yellowing of the skin and eyes.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects: Herbal comfrey tincture's antioxidant properties help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the liver, which can alleviate symptoms of jaundice such as itching and yellowing of the skin and eyes.

Stimulating liver function and regeneration: Comfrey contains compounds that stimulate liver cell regeneration and improve liver function, which can help reduce bilirubin levels and alleviate jaundice symptoms by supporting the body's natural detoxification processes.

Soothing and protecting mucous membranes: Herbal comfrey tincture's anti-inflammatory properties also soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the digestive tract and biliary system, which can help reduce inflammation and discomfort associated with jaundice.
remedy_parts: Roots: Comfrey roots are rich in allantoin, a natural anti-inflammatory that helps to soothe and protect liver tissue, making them ideal for treating jaundice.
Barks: The barks of comfrey contain a high concentration of saponins, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and improve bile flow, making them effective for treating jaundiced skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality symphytum officinale roots and clean them thoroughly.
Cut the cleaned roots into small pieces and dry them in a warm oven at 150°f for 4 hours.
Grind the dried root pieces into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Combine 1 part of the powdered symphytum officinale with 2 parts of 95% ethanol in a glass jar.
Allow the mixture to steep at room temperature for 6 weeks before straining and filtering.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold tinctures helps with jaundice because they contain a unique combination of antioxidants, anti-inflammatory compounds, and flavonoids that work together to alleviate symptoms. The tannins present in the tincture help reduce inflammation and ease discomfort, while the carotenoids have been shown to improve liver function and enhance bile flow, ultimately helping to eliminate bilirubin from the body. Additionally, the antimicrobial properties of pot marigold may also aid in preventing secondary infections that can complicate jaundice treatment.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Pot marigold's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce swelling and irritation in the liver, which can be beneficial for individuals suffering from jaundice.
Antioxidant: The antioxidant properties of pot marigold can help protect the liver cells from damage caused by free radicals, reducing the risk of further liver damage and promoting healing.

Choleretic: Pot marigold's choleretic properties can stimulate the production of bile in the liver, which can help to break down bilirubin and reduce the amount of bile that builds up in the bloodstream, a common symptom of jaundice.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of pot marigold are used to treat jaundice due to their ability to stimulate liver function and improve digestion, helping to alleviate symptoms such as yellowing of the skin and eyes.

Seeds: Pot marigold seeds are used in traditional medicine to treat jaundice because they contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce inflammation and ease discomfort associated with liver disorders.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh calendula flowers at peak bloom and dry them using desiccant or air-drying methods.
Combine one part dried calendula flowers with two parts high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Allow the mixture to steep for 4-6 weeks in a cool dark place, shaking the jar daily.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth and discard the solids; transfer the liquid to a dark glass bottle.
Store the final tincture in the refrigerator at a temperature of 35°f to 40°f for up to one year.

intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for jaundice are concentrated plant extracts that help alleviate symptoms and support liver function, thereby reducing jaundice severity. The best herbal tinctures for jaundice include Silybum marianum, Taraxacum officinale, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Artemisia absinthium, Curcuma longa, Cynara scolymus, Chelidonium majus, Berberis vulgaris, Arctium lappa, and Phyllanthus niruri. If left untreated, jaundice can lead to severe health complications and negatively impact daily life, causing fatigue, weight loss, and emotional distress, ultimately affecting relationships, work, and overall well-being.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to jaundice that are treated with herbal tinctures are liver and gallbladder issues, such as hepatitis, cirrhosis, and cholangitis. Herbal tinctures like milk thistle (Silybum marianum), dandelion (Taraxacum officinale), and turmeric (Curcuma longa) have been traditionally used to support liver function and alleviate symptoms of these conditions. Other ailments that may be treated with herbal tinctures include gallstone formation, digestive issues, and skin problems caused by liver dysfunction.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal tinctures that help with jaundice is a blend of Milk Thistle, Dandelion, and Turmeric. Milk Thistle, rich in silymarin, supports liver function and regeneration, while Dandelion root tincture aids in detoxification and bile production. Turmeric's curcumin has potent anti-inflammatory properties that reduce liver inflammation and promote healing. Combine 10-15 drops of each tincture in water or juice, 2-3 times a day, under the guidance of a healthcare professional, to support liver health and alleviate jaundice symptoms.

remedies.remedy_desc: Silybum marianum tinctures helps with jaundice because they contain silymarin, a bioflavonoid complex that supports liver health. The liver plays a crucial role in removing bilirubin, a pigment responsible for jaundice. By promoting liver regeneration and improving its function, silymarin reduces bilirubin levels and alleviates jaundice symptoms. Additionally, silymarin's antioxidant properties help neutralize free radicals that can damage liver cells, further contributing to its beneficial effects in treating jaundice. This leads to a faster recovery and improved overall health.
remedy_constituents: Silymarin: Acts as an antioxidant and anti-inflammatory, helping to reduce liver damage and promote liver cell regeneration, alleviating jaundice symptoms.
Silibinin: Exhibits hepatoprotective properties, inhibiting the formation of bilirubin and promoting its excretion, thereby reducing jaundice severity.
Isosilicristin: Demonstrates antioxidant activity, scavenging free radicals and reducing oxidative stress in the liver, which can contribute to jaundice development.
remedy_parts: Seeds: The seeds of Silybum marianum are commonly used to make tinctures due to their high concentration of silymarin, a flavonoid complex that has been shown to have hepatoprotective properties.
Leaves: The leaves of the plant are also used to make tinctures, as they contain silymarin and other bioactive compounds that may help alleviate jaundice symptoms.
Flowers: The flowers of Silybum marianum are used to make tinctures, as they contain silymarin and other flavonoids that may have hepatoprotective and anti-inflammatory effects.
remedy_recipe: Harvest silybum marianum flowers and leaves by cutting them at the base of the plant early in the morning.
Dry the plant material in a warm dark place for 2 to 4 weeks to prevent mold.
Combine 100 grams of the dried plant material with 500 milliliters of 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks shaking the jar every day.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass container discarding the solids.

remedies.remedy_desc: Taraxacum officinale tinctures helps with jaundice because of its potent liver-protective properties. The active compounds in Taraxacum officinale, such as sesquiterpene lactones and flavonoids, have been shown to stimulate liver function, promote bile production, and enhance the removal of bilirubin from the bloodstream. This natural remedy has been traditionally used to support liver health and alleviate symptoms of jaundice, including jaundice in newborns. Its anti-inflammatory effects also help to reduce liver congestion and promote a healthy liver environment.
remedy_constituents: Furocoumarins: These compounds have been found to have hepatoprotective effects, which can help alleviate liver damage and promote liver function, reducing jaundice symptoms.
Flavonoids: Flavonoids in Taraxacum officinale have antioxidant properties, which can help reduce oxidative stress in the liver, alleviate jaundice, and support overall liver health.
Saponins: Saponins in Taraxacum officinale may help improve liver function and reduce inflammation, which can help alleviate jaundice symptoms and promote overall liver health.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They contain taraxasterol, which is believed to have anti-inflammatory properties that may help alleviate jaundice symptoms.
Roots: The roots are thought to contain saponins and flavonoids, which may help to stimulate liver function and reduce jaundice.
Buds: The buds of Taraxacum officinale may contain compounds that help to reduce liver inflammation and promote bile production.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1-2 pounds of fresh or dried plant material and clean it thoroughly with a soft brush to remove dirt.
Chop the plant material into small pieces and combine it with 50-75% vodka or 80-100 proof grain alcohol in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture for 2-6 weeks in a cool dark place with occasional shaking to ensure even extraction of plant properties.
Strain the liquid mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass container to separate plant material.
Store the tincture in a cool dark place for at least 2 weeks before use to allow flavors to mature completely.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra tinctures helps with jaundice because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The root of the licorice plant, Glycyrrhiza glabra, is known to stimulate liver function and promote the removal of bilirubin from the blood, a key component in jaundice. By reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the liver, Glycyrrhiza glabra tinctures may help alleviate jaundice symptoms, such as yellowing of the skin and eyes, and support overall liver health. This natural remedy has been used for centuries to aid in liver recovery and rejuvenation.
remedy_constituents: Glycyrrhizin: Helps with jaundice by reducing liver inflammation and promoting the elimination of bilirubin, a key component of bile responsible for jaundice.
Isoliquiritigenin: Contributes to jaundice relief by inhibiting the production of bilirubin and promoting its excretion, thus reducing the severity of jaundice.
Licoricidin: Plays a role in jaundice treatment by inhibiting the formation of bilirubin and other bile pigments, which helps in reducing jaundice symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are the primary source of glycyrrhizin, a compound that has been shown to help alleviate jaundice symptoms.
Leaves: The leaves of Glycyrrhiza glabra contain flavonoids and saponins, which may help reduce bilirubin levels and alleviate jaundice symptoms.
Barks: The barks of the plant contain glycosides, which may help reduce inflammation and promote liver function, alleviating jaundice symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried roots of the plant in quantities of one part to three parts solvent at a ratio.
Chop the harvested roots into small pieces to increase the surface area for solvent extraction.
Combine the chopped roots with the solvent in a clean glass container to create the tincture.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 weeks with occasional shaking and stirring.
Strain and press the tincture to separate the liquid from the solids and discard the solids.

remedies.remedy_desc: Artemisia absinthium tinctures helps with jaundice because it supports liver health and function. The plant contains sesquiterpene lactones, which have been shown to have hepatoprotective properties, helping to protect liver cells from damage. Artemisia absinthium also contains antioxidants that neutralize free radicals, reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the liver. As a result, it may help to alleviate symptoms of jaundice, such as yellowing of the skin and eyes, and promote a return to normal liver function. This may also aid in the resolution of underlying liver conditions.
remedy_constituents: Thujone: Acts as a hepatoprotective agent, helping to protect the liver from damage and promote the regeneration of liver cells, which is beneficial in treating jaundice caused by liver dysfunction.
Absinthin: Exhibits antioxidant properties, which help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the liver, thereby alleviating jaundice symptoms and promoting liver health.

Perillyl alcohol: Demonstrates choleretic properties, stimulating the liver to increase bile production and promote the flow of bile into the intestines, which helps to relieve jaundice symptoms by removing bilirubin from the body.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Artemisia absinthium leaves are commonly used due to their bitter compounds that are believed to stimulate liver function and reduce jaundice symptoms.
Stems: Artemisia absinthium stems are used for their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate liver inflammation associated with jaundice.
Flowers: Artemisia absinthium flowers are used for their bitter and antiseptic properties, which can help stimulate digestion and reduce liver congestion.
remedy_recipe: Gather 250 grams of fresh artemisia absinthium leaves and flowers in the early morning or afternoon.
Chop the plant material into small pieces using sharp scissors or a sharp knife.
Mix the chopped plant material with 500 milliliters of 45% ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks, shaking it every day.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle.

remedies.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa tinctures helps with jaundice because of its potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce bilirubin levels in the blood. The active compound curcumin present in it has been shown to promote the excretion of bilirubin through the kidneys, thereby reducing the severity of jaundice. Additionally, it helps to reduce liver inflammation and oxidative stress, which are often associated with jaundice. This natural remedy may aid in alleviating symptoms and promoting overall liver health.
remedy_constituents: Curcumin: It helps with jaundice by reducing liver inflammation and oxidative stress, which can contribute to the development of jaundice.
Demethoxycurcumin: It aids in jaundice by inhibiting the production of bilirubin, a pigment responsible for the yellow color associated with jaundice.
Bisdemethoxycurcumin: It helps alleviate jaundice by reducing the liver's uptake of bilirubin, thereby decreasing its concentration in the blood.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: They are the primary part used due to their high concentration of curcumin, a compound known for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
Roots: Roots are also used as an alternative to rhyzomes and contain similar compounds, making them effective in treating jaundice.
Leaves: Leaves are sometimes used to make tinctures, particularly in traditional medicine, as they are believed to have similar health benefits to the rhyzomes and roots.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh curcuma longa roots in the morning after the dew has evaporated using scissors.
Clean and dry the roots thoroughly then chop them into small pieces using a knife.
Combine the chopped root pieces with 80% vodka by volume in a clean glass jar.
Seal the jar and store it in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into another clean glass bottle discard the solids.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cynara scolymus tinctures helps with jaundice because it supports the liver's natural detoxification processes. This herbal remedy, also known as artichoke, contains compounds that stimulate bile production and flow, which can help reduce bilirubin levels associated with jaundice. By promoting the elimination of toxins and waste products, Cynara scolymus tinctures may alleviate symptoms of jaundice and support overall liver health. Its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties may also contribute to a faster recovery and improved overall well-being.
remedy_constituents: Phenolic acids: Help reduce liver enzymes and improve liver function, which can alleviate jaundice symptoms.
Terpenoids: Exhibit hepatoprotective properties, reducing liver damage and promoting the regeneration of liver cells, thereby helping to treat jaundice.
Flavonoids: Possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help in protecting the liver from damage and improving its overall function, ultimately reducing jaundice symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They contain sesquiterpene lactones which may have hepatoprotective effects and help alleviate jaundice symptoms.
Flowers: The flowers are rich in flavonoids and saponins, which may help reduce liver inflammation and improve liver function.
Stems: The stems of Cynara scolymus are a rich source of inulin, a prebiotic that can help support the growth of beneficial gut bacteria, potentially aiding in the treatment of jaundice.
remedy_recipe: Harvest cynara scolymus flowers in full bloom and dry them for 2-3 weeks to preserve their potency.
Combine 1 part dried cynara scolymus flowers with 2 parts vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-3 weeks shaking the jar every few days.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar.
Store the tincture in a cool dark place for 4-6 weeks before using it to allow it to mature.

remedies.remedy_desc: Chelidonium majus tinctures helps with jaundice because it is believed to support liver function and promote the removal of bile. The herb contains chelidonine, a compound that may help to stimulate the liver's natural detoxification processes and improve the flow of bile. Additionally, Chelidonium majus has anti-inflammatory properties, which may help to reduce inflammation and swelling in the liver and gallbladder, allowing for proper bile flow and reducing jaundice symptoms. This can lead to improved liver health and reduced jaundice severity.
remedy_constituents: Alkaloids: Alkaloids in Chelidonium majus tinctures help with jaundice by stimulating liver function and promoting the elimination of bilirubin from the body.
Flavonoids: Flavonoids in Chelidonium majus tinctures help with jaundice by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the liver, which can contribute to jaundice.

Sesquiterpenes: Sesquiterpenes in Chelidonium majus tinctures help with jaundice by exhibiting hepatoprotective properties, protecting the liver cells from damage and promoting its normal functioning.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Chelidonium majus are used for making tinctures because they contain the highest concentration of alkaloids, such as chelidonine and sanguinarine, which have hepatoprotective properties.
Leaves: The leaves of Chelidonium majus are used for making tinctures because they contain bioactive compounds that help in the treatment of liver and gallbladder disorders, including jaundice.
Buds: The buds of Chelidonium majus are used for making tinctures because they contain a high concentration of alkaloids, similar to the roots, which help in treating liver and gallbladder problems.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of dried chelidonium majus roots in september or october when the plant is mature.
Chop 1 part of dried chelidonium majus roots into small pieces to increase surface area.
Combine 1 part of chopped chelidonium majus roots with 2 parts of 80-proof vodka in a glass jar.
Steep the mixture for 2 weeks in a cool dark place with occasional shaking.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter and discard the solids after 2 weeks of steeping.

remedies.remedy_desc: Berberis vulgaris tinctures helps with jaundice because they contain berberine, a bioactive compound that has potent hepatoprotective and choleretic properties. The tincture stimulates the liver to increase bile production, which helps to remove bilirubin from the blood, alleviating jaundice symptoms. Additionally, berberine has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects that help to soothe liver tissues and promote overall liver health. This leads to improved liver function, reduced bilirubin levels, and a decrease in jaundice symptoms, making Berberis vulgaris tinctures a valuable natural remedy for jaundice.
remedy_constituents: Berberine: It helps with jaundice by reducing liver inflammation and improving liver function, thereby aiding in the removal of bilirubin from the body.
Oleanolic acid: It helps with jaundice by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes in the liver, which can cause damage and contribute to the condition.
Vitamin B12 analogues: They help with jaundice by enhancing the production of red blood cells, which can be reduced in cases of jaundice, and thereby improving overall health.
remedy_parts: Roots: Used due to their high content of berberine, a compound believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that help alleviate jaundice symptoms.
Fruits: Used because they contain berberine and other alkaloids that have been shown to have hepatoprotective effects, helping to reduce liver inflammation associated with jaundice.
Leaves: Used for their berberine content, which has been found to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help alleviate jaundice symptoms and protect the liver from damage.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1 part of fresh or dried berberis vulgaris root and 2 parts of a neutral solvent.
Chop the berberis vulgaris root into small pieces and add it to a clean glass jar.
Fill the jar with the solvent to cover the root and seal the jar tightly.
Place the jar in a cool dark place and steep it for 2-6 weeks.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth and discard the solids to obtain the tincture.

remedies.remedy_desc: Arctium lappa tinctures helps with jaundice because of its unique properties. The tincture is rich in saponins, which are potent antioxidants that help reduce liver inflammation and promote healthy liver function. The active compounds in Arctium lappa, such as inulin and arctiin, also exhibit anti-inflammatory and antiviral activities, further supporting liver health. By reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the liver, Arctium lappa tinctures may help alleviate jaundice symptoms, promoting a more efficient liver and overall well-being.
remedy_constituents: Lignans: Help with jaundice by inhibiting the activity of certain enzymes involved in the production and transport of bilirubin.
Saponins: Assist in jaundice treatment by reducing the amount of bilirubin in the blood and improving its excretion.
Polysaccharides: Contribute to the alleviation of jaundice symptoms by enhancing the liver's ability to detoxify and eliminate toxins, including bilirubin.
remedy_parts: Roots: Used for their bitter and anthraquinone content, which helps to stimulate liver function and reduce jaundice.
Seeds: Used to support liver detoxification and reduce inflammation, promoting relief from jaundice symptoms.
Leaves: Utilized for their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to soothe and protect the liver, alleviating jaundice.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of the roots and 2 parts of the aerial parts of arctium lappa in the fall.
Dry the harvested parts in a warm place with good air circulation for 1 week.
Chop the dried roots and aerial parts into small pieces and fill a clean glass jar about half full.
Add 70% ethanol or vodka in a 1:3 ratio to the chopped plant material and seal the jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-6 weeks shaking the jar every day.

remedies.remedy_desc: Phyllanthus niruri tinctures helps with jaundice because it has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to support liver health and reduce bilirubin levels. The herb is believed to have hepatoprotective properties, which help to regenerate liver cells and improve liver function. Studies have also shown that Phyllanthus niruri has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce liver inflammation and oxidative stress associated with jaundice. This makes it a potential natural remedy for jaundice.
remedy_constituents: Phenolic acids: They help reduce bilirubin levels in the blood by promoting its excretion and preventing its reabsorption.
Flavonoids: They have antioxidant properties that help protect liver cells from damage and promote liver regeneration, aiding in the recovery from jaundice.
Quinones: They have hepatoprotective effects by reducing liver inflammation and oxidative stress, which helps alleviate jaundice symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in active compounds like phyllanthin and hypophyllanthin, which are known for their hepatoprotective and antioxidant properties, helping to treat jaundice.
Stems: The stems of Phyllanthus niruri contain flavonoids, glycosides, and alkaloids, which exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, supporting liver health and treating jaundice.

Fruits: The fruits of Phyllanthus niruri are rich in flavonoids, saponins, and other bioactive compounds, which have been found to have hepatoprotective and antioxidant properties, helping to alleviate jaundice symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh phyllanthus niruri leaves and stems in the early morning when they are at their most potent and effective.
Clean and dry the harvested plant material in a low-temperature oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit for two hours.
Combine 1 part of the dried plant material with 2 parts of vodka in a clean glass jar to create the tincture.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for four weeks shaking the jar daily to facilitate extraction.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter and discard the solids to obtain the final product.
